title: When Things Break 💥
description: Understand what Exceptions are and why they crash your program.

slides.title: The Infinite Sadness 😭
slides.content:
No matter how perfect you are, your programs will eventually crash.

Maybe a user inputs a word instead of a number. Maybe the database server goes offline.

When a program encounters something it cannot physically compute, it panics and throws an **Exception**.

slides.title: What is an Exception?
slides.content:
An Exception is literally just an Object (like a String or Integer) that Ruby creates out of thin air to represent the error.

If an Exception is created and not immediately handled, Ruby instantly terminates the entire program to protect itself!

slides.title: The Crash
slides.content: Run this code! You cannot divide a number by zero. Ruby panics, generates a `ZeroDivisionError` Exception object, and instantly kills the script.
slides.codeSnippet:
puts "1. Starting math..."

# This immediately crashes the program!
result = 10 / 0

# This will never print because the program is dead
puts "2. Finished math!"

quizData.question: What actually IS an Exception in Ruby?

options.text: A physical Object that Ruby instantiates to officially represent an error state.

quizData.explanation: Because "Everything is an Object" in Ruby, even the errors themselves are just shiny objects created from Error classes!

slides.title: Common Exceptions 🐛
slides.content:
There is an entire massive family tree of Exception classes built perfectly into Ruby.

- `NoMethodError`: Tried to run a method that doesn't exist.
- `ArgumentError`: Passed 2 arguments to a method requiring 3.
- `TypeError`: Tried to add `1 + "Hello"`.

quizData.question: If an Exception is 'raised' (created) and your code doesn't do anything to handle it, what does Ruby do by default?

options.text: It silently ignores the line and continues.
It instantly terminates the absolute entire program right there to prevent unpredictable behavior.

quizData.explanation: Unhandled exceptions are fatal. They instantly bring the entire server down.
